mnemonics: Greedy Dragons Pick Coins Carefully (Greedy, DP, P vs NP, Complexity Classes)
Divide & Conquer Like A King (Merge Sort, Quick Sort, Recurrences)
NP-Hard? No Problem, Verify Carefully (NP-Completeness, Reductions, Verification)
keyPoints: Understand the key differences between Greedy algorithms and Dynamic Programming (local vs. global optimum).
Be able to solve recurrence relations for Divide and Conquer algorithms using the Master Theorem or iteration.
Know how to prove a problem is NP-Complete by reducing a known NP-Complete problem to it.
commonMistakes: Confusing when to use Memoization (top-down DP) versus Tabulation (bottom-up DP).
Incorrectly applying the Master Theorem by misidentifying the values of a, b, and f(n).
Attempting to prove NP-Completeness by reducing the new problem *to* the known problem, instead of the correct direction (known problem *to* the new problem).
